client-only.title: Client Only Mode
client-only.description: Client Only Mode is designed for RX machines in a network forwarding setup. When enabled, Asteriq hides the Devices and Mappings tabs, leaving only the SC Bindings and Settings tabs visible.

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: The Devices tab is the starting point for any Asteriq setup. It shows all connected physical controllers and your configured vJoy virtual devices, and is where you assign physical inputs to virtual outputs and start or stop forwarding.

getting-started.title: Getting Started
getting-started.description: This page walks you through setting up Asteriq from scratch — from installation to your first forwarding session.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: Asteriq is a HOTAS management application for Star Citizen. It brings device assignment, axis curve configuration, Star Citizen bindings, and network input forwarding into one window.

mappings.title: Mappings
mappings.description: The Mappings tab is where you configure how each physical input is transformed before it reaches the virtual device. Axis response curves, deadzones, button modes, and multi-axis merging are all configured here.

network-forwarding.title: Network Forwarding
network-forwarding.description: Network forwarding lets you use your HOTAS setup from a different PC on your local network. The machine with the physical controllers transmits input to another machine, which presents it as virtual joystick devices to Star Citizen.

sc-bindings.title: SC Bindings
sc-bindings.description: The SC Bindings tab lets you load a Star Citizen control profile, assign inputs to SC actions across your devices, detect binding conflicts, and export a ready-to-use profile to your Star Citizen directory.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: The Settings tab covers configuration management, system driver status, network forwarding setup, visual themes, and general application preferences.
